articles.title: Trump reinstates Iran port blockade and vows 20% charge on cargo passing through Hormuz
articles.summary: The US military later said it had started the third consecutive night of strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to run to safety
articles.summary: Malcolm Timbrell's wife and their friends are thought to have died when a devastating wildfire tore through their village in Spain.

articles.title: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
articles.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

articles.title: At least 28 killed, 25 critically injured after fire engulfs Bangkok bar
articles.summary: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

articles.summary: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.

articles.title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
articles.summary: The interior minister has said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested.

articles.title: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
articles.summary: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

articles.title: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
articles.summary: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".

articles.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
articles.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

articles.title: Sudan's paramilitary RSF chief sentenced to death over war crimes
articles.summary: Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo was convicted in absentia along with 15 RSF commanders and allies.
